findings.title: Repeated try/finally cleanup pattern across 8 test cases
findings.description: Every test in this file wraps its body in an identical try/finally block calling await cleanup(). This pattern is repeated 8 times across 180 lines. The cleanup logic (closing client and server) is a natural fit for afterEach, which Vitest already supports and which would eliminate the boilerplate entirely while also ensuring cleanup runs even if the test setup fails after createConnectedPair.

findings.title: Near-duplicate registry+channels setup across 5 test cases
findings.description: The pattern of creating a ChannelRegistry, defining the 5 platforms array, creating stub channels, and registering them is repeated nearly identically in 5 tests (lines 77-84, 93-102, 117-126, 143-152, 196-203). The variation is small: some tests need the channels array, some need a Map. A shared helper would reduce duplication without sacrificing readability.

findings.title: Single test case spans 78 lines with 15+ assertions
findings.description: This test verifies structure, source count, entity deduplication, keyword deduplication, importance averaging, and activity classification -- all in one 78-line test. While it is an integration checkpoint (so breadth is intentional), the test is harder to diagnose on failure because a single failure message does not indicate which aspect broke. Consider splitting into 3-4 focused tests: (1) structure validation, (2) entity deduplication, (3) keyword deduplication, (4) importance/activity classification.

findings.title: StubLlmProvider dispatches on schemaDescription string content
findings.description: The stub dispatches its return value by checking if the schemaDescription string includes 'persons'. This couples the test double to an implementation detail of the prompt text. If the schema description wording changes (e.g., 'people' instead of 'persons'), the stub silently returns the wrong data. Consider using a call counter or explicit method name/schema key for dispatch.

positive_observations: All test files use well-named factory functions (createStubChannel, makeSource, makeTriggerInput, createMemory, etc.) with sensible defaults and override patterns -- clean and readable
persona-engine tests use data-driven test generation for 11 trigger scenarios and 7 activity types, keeping the test DRY without sacrificing clarity
Test Double rationale comments are present in every file that uses stubs, documenting why real dependencies are not used
Function lengths are generally well-controlled: most factory functions are under 30 lines, most tests under 30 lines
channels-extra test covers defensive behaviors (duplicate registration, platform mismatch, undefined lookup) thoroughly
stage-ui tests cleanly verify store isolation -- mutations in one store do not affect others
